name: Korean Beef Bowl
steps: Cook the rice per package directions.
Heat sesame oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ginger and garlic, cooking 20-30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the beef, breaking it apart, and brown for 5-6 minutes.
Add the soy sauce and brown sugar, tossing to coat, and cook 2 more minutes until the sauce thickens slightly and clings to the beef.
Serve over rice, topped with scallions.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps_detailed: Cook the rice per package directions.
Heat sesame oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ginger and garlic, cooking 20-30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the beef, breaking it apart, and brown for 5-6 minutes.
Add the soy sauce and brown sugar, tossing to coat, and cook 2 more minutes until the sauce thickens slightly and clings to the beef.
Serve over rice, topped with scallions.
description: Swapped the rice for real short-grain Korean-style rice - Korean cooking wants a stickier, shorter grain than basmati, jasmine, or brown rice.

name: Chicken Bulgogi

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps: Marinate and grill the chicken.
Serve over the rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: McCormick Sesame Oil, Aceite De Atonjoli
365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps_detailed: Marinate the chicken in the soy sauce, mirin, garlic, ginger, and sesame oil for at least 20 minutes, ideally longer.
Grill or pan-sear over high heat, 2-3 minutes per side, until charred at the edges and cooked through.
Cook the rice separately and serve with the kimchi alongside.
description: Swapped basmati for real short-grain Korean-style rice. Real bulgogi is also traditionally made with grated Asian pear or apple worked into the marinade, which tenderizes the meat and adds a specific sweetness - that's genuinely missing here, though the core soy-garlic-sesame marinade direction is otherwise correct.

name: Gochujang Salmon
steps: Whisk the gochujang, honey, and soy sauce together for the glaze.
Preheat the oven to 400F. Brush the salmon generously with the glaze and bake 12-15 minutes until it flakes easily.
Meanwhile, cook the rice per package directions, and steam or saute the broccoli with sesame oil until tender-crisp, about 5-6 minutes.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps_detailed: Whisk the gochujang, honey, and soy sauce together for the glaze.
Preheat the oven to 400F. Brush the salmon generously with the glaze and bake 12-15 minutes until it flakes easily.
Meanwhile, cook the rice per package directions, and steam or saute the broccoli with sesame oil until tender-crisp, about 5-6 minutes.
description: Swapped the rice for real short-grain Korean-style rice - Korean cooking wants a stickier, shorter grain than basmati, jasmine, or brown rice.

name: Korean Tofu Stir-Fry
steps: Pat the tofu very dry — this is essential for getting a good sear rather than steaming it.
Heat a splash of oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and sear the tofu 3-4 minutes per side until golden.
Whisk the gochujang and soy sauce together and add to the pan along with the garlic, tossing to coat the tofu.
Add the bok choy and stir-fry 2-3 minutes until wilted.
Cook the rice per package directions and serve everything over it, topped with sesame seeds.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps_detailed: Pat the tofu very dry — this is essential for getting a good sear rather than steaming it.
Heat a splash of oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and sear the tofu 3-4 minutes per side until golden.
Whisk the gochujang and soy sauce together and add to the pan along with the garlic, tossing to coat the tofu.

name: Korean Chicken & Kimchi Jar
steps: Toss the chicken with sesame oil.
Layer the rice, kimchi, and chicken in a jar.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps_detailed: Toss the chicken with sesame oil.
Layer the rice, kimchi, and chicken in a jar.
description: Swapped the rice for real short-grain Korean-style rice - Korean cooking wants a stickier, shorter grain than basmati, jasmine, or brown rice.

name: Korean Beef Bulgogi Bowl
steps: Whisk the soy sauce, honey, garlic, and sesame oil together. Toss with the beef and marinate at least 20 minutes.
Cook the rice per package directions.
Heat a skillet over high heat and sear the beef in batches, 1-2 minutes per side, until charred at the edges and cooked through.
Serve over rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps_detailed: Whisk the soy sauce, honey, garlic, and sesame oil together. Toss with the beef and marinate at least 20 minutes.
Cook the rice per package directions.
Heat a skillet over high heat and sear the beef in batches, 1-2 minutes per side, until charred at the edges and cooked through.
Serve over rice.
description: Swapped the rice for real short-grain Korean-style rice - Korean cooking wants a stickier, shorter grain than basmati, jasmine, or brown rice.

name: Spicy Korean Pork Bowl
steps: Whisk the gochujang, soy sauce, honey, and garlic together. Toss with the pork and marinate at least 20 minutes.
Cook the rice per package directions.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and cook the pork 6-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until cooked through and the sauce has thickened into a glaze.
Serve over rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps_detailed: Whisk the gochujang, soy sauce, honey, and garlic together. Toss with the pork and marinate at least 20 minutes.
Cook the rice per package directions.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and cook the pork 6-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until cooked through and the sauce has thickened into a glaze.
Serve over rice.
description: Swapped the rice for real short-grain Korean-style rice - Korean cooking wants a stickier, shorter grain than basmati, jasmine, or brown rice.

name: Korean Fried Chicken
steps: Coat the chicken in cornstarch and fry once, 8-10 minutes.
Toss in the gochujang-honey sauce.
steps_detailed: Toss the chicken pieces in the cornstarch to coat.
Fry in the oil at 325F for 6-7 minutes until cooked through but pale; remove and rest 5 minutes.
Increase the oil to 375F and fry the chicken again, 2-3 minutes, until deeply golden and audibly crackling-crisp.
Whisk the gochujang, honey, soy sauce, and garlic together and warm gently in a separate pan, 1-2 minutes.
Toss the double-fried chicken in the sauce just before serving.
description: Real Korean fried chicken is specifically double-fried, which is why it stays shatteringly crisp even under sauce, unlike most fried chicken - baking can't replicate that.

name: Doenjang Vegetable Stew
steps: Whisk the miso paste into 1.5 cups warm water until dissolved.
Simmer the onion and garlic in the broth for 5 minutes.
Add the zucchini and simmer 6-8 minutes until tender.
Add the tofu and warm through gently for 2-3 minutes.
Cook the rice per package directions and serve alongside.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps_detailed: Whisk the miso paste into 1.5 cups warm water until dissolved.
Simmer the onion and garlic in the broth for 5 minutes.
Add the zucchini and simmer 6-8 minutes until tender.
Add the tofu and warm through gently for 2-3 minutes.
Cook the rice per package directions and serve alongside.

name: Korean Beef & Rice Jar
steps: Toss the cooked beef with soy sauce and honey.
Layer the rice, carrot, and beef in a jar.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps_detailed: Toss the cooked beef with soy sauce and honey.
Layer the rice, carrot, and beef in a jar.
description: Swapped the rice for real short-grain Korean-style rice - Korean cooking wants a stickier, shorter grain than basmati, jasmine, or brown rice.

name: Bibimbap Bowl

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps: Cook the beef.
Toss the vegetables together.
Serve over rice with the gochujang and a fried egg.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice
McCormick Sesame Oil, Aceite De Atonjoli

steps_detailed: Season the beef with a little soy sauce and cook through in a hot pan, 5-6 minutes.
Blanch the spinach briefly, squeeze dry, and toss with a few drops of sesame oil and a pinch of minced garlic.
Blanch the bean sprouts 2 minutes, then toss the same way.
Quickly sauté the carrot in a little oil, 2 minutes, just until slightly softened.
Cook the rice, then arrange it in a bowl with the beef and each vegetable in its own distinct section.
Top with the fried egg and a generous dollop of gochujang.
description: Real bibimbap is built on each vegetable being separately blanched or sautéed and seasoned on its own, then arranged in distinct sections around the bowl, mixed together at the table - which is what the name literally means ('mixed rice') - rather than combined into one pile before serving. Served over real short-grain rice.

name: Korean Tofu Lettuce Wraps
steps: Cook tofu with soy sauce and gochujang.
Serve in lettuce leaves.
steps_detailed: Sear the well-dried tofu in a lightly oiled skillet, 3-4 minutes per side until golden.
Whisk the soy sauce, gochujang, and garlic together and toss with the tofu.
Spoon into lettuce leaves to serve.

name: Korean Egg Rice Breakfast
steps: Cook the rice per package directions.
Fry the eggs in sesame oil to your preference.
Serve the eggs over the rice, drizzled with soy sauce and topped with scallions.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps_detailed: Cook the rice per package directions.
Fry the eggs in sesame oil to your preference.
Serve the eggs over the rice, drizzled with soy sauce and topped with scallions.
description: Swapped the rice for real short-grain Korean-style rice - Korean cooking wants a stickier, shorter grain than basmati, jasmine, or brown rice.

name: Kimchi Fried Rice Snack Cup
steps: Heat sesame oil in a small pan over medium-high heat.
Add the rice and kimchi, stir-frying 3-4 minutes until heated through and slightly crisp at the edges.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Short Grain White Sushi Rice

steps_detailed: Heat sesame oil in a small pan over medium-high heat.
Add the rice and kimchi, stir-frying 3-4 minutes until heated through and slightly crisp at the edges.
description: Swapped the rice for real short-grain Korean-style rice - Korean cooking wants a stickier, shorter grain than basmati, jasmine, or brown rice.

name: Roasted Korean Sweet Potato
steps: Toss sweet potato with sesame oil.
Roast at 425F for 20-25 min.
steps_detailed: Preheat the oven to 425F.
Toss the sweet potato with sesame oil.
Roast 20-25 minutes, flipping halfway, until tender and caramelized at the edges.

name: Korean Cinnamon Rice Cake (Sweet)
steps: Mix the batter and steam in a dish for 25-30 minutes until set.

ingredients_full.note: ground into flour, or use store-bought sweet rice flour

steps_detailed: Whisk the glutinous rice flour, cinnamon, sugar, and milk into a smooth, thick batter.
Pour into a small greased dish and steam over simmering water 25-30 minutes until firm and set all the way through.
Cool slightly, slice, and top with the pistachios.
description: Korean tteok rice cakes, sweet or savory, are built on glutinous rice flour specifically, the same starch that gives mochi its chew - a wheat-based cake would have a completely different, more cake-like texture. Cinnamon and sugar together is a genuine, traditional Korean sweet flavor pairing.

name: Gochujang Glaze
steps: Whisk all ingredients together.
steps_detailed: Whisk all ingredients together. Brush onto grilled or roasted meat in the last few minutes of cooking so it caramelizes without burning.

name: Korean Turkey Lettuce Cups
steps: Brown turkey, stir in gochujang and soy sauce.
Serve in lettuce leaves.
steps_detailed: Brown the turkey with garlic in a skillet over medium-high heat, 6-7 minutes, breaking it apart.
Stir in gochujang and soy sauce, cooking 1-2 more minutes until well coated.
Spoon into lettuce leaves to serve.

name: Korean Cucumber Banchan Salad
steps: Toss cucumber with sesame oil and gochugaru.
Serve alongside chickpeas for protein.
steps_detailed: Toss the cucumber with sesame oil, gochugaru, and garlic.
Let sit 5-10 minutes to lightly pickle and soften. Serve alongside the chickpeas.

name: Korean BBQ Meatball Snack Cup
steps: Pan-sear meatballs until cooked through.
Toss with soy sauce and honey.
steps_detailed: Mix the beef with garlic and shape into small meatballs.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and cook the meatballs 6-8 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned and cooked through.
Add the soy sauce and honey in the last 2 minutes, tossing to glaze.

name: Korean Beef Salad Bowl
steps: Whisk soy sauce and sesame oil.
Toss greens and cucumber with dressing, top with beef.
steps_detailed: Whisk the soy sauce and sesame oil together.
Toss the greens and cucumber with the dressing, top with sliced beef.

name: Kimchi Jjigae (Kimchi Stew)
steps: Brown the pork.
Add the kimchi and broth, and simmer 15 minutes.
Add the tofu.
steps_detailed: Brown the pork with the garlic, 5-6 minutes.
Add the kimchi and gochugaru, and cook 3-4 minutes to let the kimchi's flavor deepen and slightly caramelize at the edges.
Add the broth and simmer 12-15 minutes to let the flavors meld.
Add the tofu and warm through gently, 3-4 minutes.
description: Added gochugaru for real color and a cleaner, fruitier heat than kimchi's own spice alone provides - genuine jjigae leans on both together. Well-fermented, properly sour kimchi, not fresh or mild kimchi, is specifically what this stew wants.
